selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a comprehensive understanding of the various types of fake content that exist, including bots, trolls, and propaganda. It is also important to recognize the reasons why people create and share fake content, such as to manipulate public opinion or to gain attention. Once you have this understanding, you can take steps to identify and flag fake content, such as using fact-checking tools or reporting suspicious activity to social media platforms. Additionally, it is important to promote critical thinking and media literacy among users, so they can better evaluate the information they encounter on social media. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a comprehensive understanding of the various types of fake content that exist, including bots, trolls, and propaganda. It is also important to recognize the reasons why people create and share fake content, such as to manipulate public opinion or to gain attention. Once you have this understanding, you can take steps to identify and flag fake content, such as using fact-checking tools or reporting suspicious activity to social media platforms. response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a comprehensive understanding of the various types of fake content that exist, including bots, trolls, and propaganda. It is also important to recognize the reasons why people create and share fake content, such as to manipulate public opinion or to gain attention. Once you have this understanding, you can take steps to identify and flag fake content, such as using fact-checking tools or reporting suspicious activity to social media platforms.
